untranslatable_reason: This signal requires Principal Component Analysis (PCA) on a cross-section of stocks to extract factor exposures and calculate expected factor-based returns. PCA is a multivariate statistical technique that operates on a matrix of stock returns and cannot be expressed using point-in-time technical indicators (RSI, moving averages, etc.). The signal's core logic—identifying principal components, ranking by eigenvalues, calculating factor exposures, and sorting stocks by expected returns—requires custom factor modeling outside the scope of standard single-security technical indicators. Additionally, the signal involves portfolio construction (long-short weighting) which is not supported by individual entry/exit conditions.
translation_notes: Signal CHAN_QT_DRY_0 implements a statistical arbitrage / factor model approach rather than a technical indicator-based trading rule. It requires: (1) cross-sectional PCA on S&P 600 constituents, (2) eigenvalue decomposition, (3) factor return forecasting with momentum assumption, (4) expected return calculations per stock. This cannot be translated into the entry/exit condition framework which assumes single-security technical analysis. Recommend implementing as custom research/portfolio construction module outside the backtest engine's rule translator.
